methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Medra, a Series A biotech company founded by Michelle Lee, is valued against a universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies spanning seed through Series B in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors to Medra's $52M total raised to date and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $104M. Peer-median analysis draws from 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M), producing a median of $1.3B with a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; the sector median P/S across the three public tickers is 6.1x, but Medra's revenue is not disclosed.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $88M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median estimate of $702M. This wide range reflects material uncertainty around Medra's clinical progress, competitive positioning within the peer set, and stage-specific financing dynamics in frontier biotech. The spread between stage-progression ($104M) and peer-median ($1.3B) suggests the key variable is whether Medra will command valuation multiples closer to earlier-stage (seed) or more developed Series B comparables.
